problem_statement: The document addresses how developers should collaborate with and oversee LLM-generated code modifications during large-scale, application-level code migration (e.g., Java 8 to Java 17) spanning entire codebases, and how much control and oversight humans want over AI output.
magnitude_evidence: QCT upgraded 1000 Java 8 applications to Java 17 in two days, with each upgrade taking 10 minutes on average, vs 2+ days previously (Amazon internal benchmark); low 4.8% accuracy of resolving GitHub issues on SWE-Bench using Claude V2
insufficiency_evidence: Participant P7 received a hallucinated output but trusted it for the whole session, trying to debug errors to no avail, not questioning the output until probed by the interviewer; 5 other participants gave up reviewing AI code and restarted the upgrade from scratch
solution_mechanisms: citations and reasoning references for changes
robust test-suite results as trust signal
user preference setting to guide AI
